channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Murder To Money Laundering: 12 Of The UK's Most Wanted Criminals Believed To Be Hiding In Spain

media:content.media:description: In the past year alone, 56 fugitives were extradited from Spain back to the UK.

item.description: Officials say Spain remains a common hiding place for British criminals because of its large UK expat population and strong travel links.
item.content:encoded: Officials say Spain remains a common hiding place for British criminals because of its large UK expat population and strong travel links.

item.title: "Make It Work And Never Mess It Up," Xi Tells Trump At Beijing Summit

media:content.media:description: Xi said the relationship between US and China can only "advance" if there is "mutual respect"

item.description: "The world is changing and turbulent," Xi said and noted that the relationship between the two countries impacts not just the 1.7 billion people who live in both countries but also the 8 billion people who live outside the two countries.
item.content:encoded: "The world is changing and turbulent," Xi said and noted that the relationship between the two countries impacts not just the 1.7 billion people who live in both countries but also the 8 billion people who live outside the two countries.

item.title: UK Health Minister Resigns, Says Lost Confidence In PM Starmer's Leadership

media:content.media:description: Streeting is popular on the right of Labour, but is disliked by MPs on the left.

item.description: Wes Streeting, 43, did not say whether he had the required support of 81 Labour MPs -- 20 per cent of the party in parliament -- to trigger a contest.
item.content:encoded: Wes Streeting, 43, did not say whether he had the required support of 81 Labour MPs -- 20 per cent of the party in parliament -- to trigger a contest.

item.title: Beijing Roast Duck To Tiramisu: Inside China's State Banquet For Trump

media:content.media:description: US President Donald Trump with Chinese President Xi Jinping at the state banquet in Beijing.

item.description: Reflecting on his talks with Xi, Trump described their discussions as "extremely positive and constructive", while thanking the Chinese leadership for what he called a "magnificent welcome" at the start of his state visit.
item.content:encoded: Reflecting on his talks with Xi, Trump described their discussions as "extremely positive and constructive", while thanking the Chinese leadership for what he called a "magnificent welcome" at the start of his state visit.

item.title: "UAE Directly Involved In Act Of Aggression Against Iran": Abbas Araghchi

media:content.media:description: Iran has repeatedly accused Gulf states of allowing US forces to carry out attacks.

item.description: Araghchi also referred to what Israel has described as a "secret" meeting in the UAE during the war between Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and UAE President Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan -- a visit Abu Dhabi has denied took place.
item.content:encoded: Araghchi also referred to what Israel has described as a "secret" meeting in the UAE during the war between Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and UAE President Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan -- a visit Abu Dhabi has denied took place.

item.title: Trump China Visit LIVE Updates: Trump Invites Xi Jinping For Visit At White House In September

media:content.media:description: Trump China Visit Live: Trade ties between Beijing and Washington have been fraught in recent years

item.description: Donald Trump's 2-Day China Visit Live Update: During the trip, US arms sales to Taiwan, China's controls on rare-earth exports, and the countries' raucous trade relationships are among the main issues expected to be taken up by Trump and Xi.
item.content:encoded: Donald Trump's 2-Day China Visit Live Update: During the trip, US arms sales to Taiwan, China's controls on rare-earth exports, and the countries' raucous trade relationships are among the main issues expected to be taken up by Trump and Xi.

item.title: Iran Allows Chinese Vessels To Pass Through Hormuz Strait: Report

media:content.media:description: Iran severely restricted transit in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.description: A Chinese supertanker carrying 2 million barrels of Iraqi crude sailed through the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded: A Chinese supertanker carrying 2 million barrels of Iraqi crude sailed through the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday.

item.title: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US And China Agree Strait Of Hormuz 'Must Remain Open'
item.description: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US President Donald Trump is set to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping today in a high-stakes summit between the two countries.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US President Donald Trump is set to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping today in a high-stakes summit between the two countries.

item.title: Why Denmark Is Less Afraid Of AI-Linked Job Losses And Can India Follow

media:content.media:description: Denmark introduced an economic model called 'flexicurity' in the 90s

item.description: Denmark's 'flexicurity' model is helping workers fear AI disruption less. Can India replicate it?
item.content:encoded: Denmark's 'flexicurity' model is helping workers fear AI disruption less. Can India replicate it?

item.title: Xi Aims To Overcome 'Thucydides Trap' In Talks With Trump: What It Means

media:content.media:description: Xi's message has been consistent over the years

item.description: Xi has mentioned the "Thucydides Trap" before with regard to US-China relations, dating back to as early as 2014. The term refers to a theory that a rising power and an established hegemon are destined for war.
item.content:encoded: Xi has mentioned the "Thucydides Trap" before with regard to US-China relations, dating back to as early as 2014. The term refers to a theory that a rising power and an established hegemon are destined for war.

item.title: 2 Academicians Go Missing In Balochistan, Search Efforts Underway

media:content.media:description: Vice Chancellor of the University of Gwadar, Prof. Dr Abdul Razzaq Sabir

item.description: Authorities have launched efforts to trace the missing individuals, as concerns continue to grow over the safety of the senior university officials.
item.content:encoded: Authorities have launched efforts to trace the missing individuals, as concerns continue to grow over the safety of the senior university officials.

item.title: At Trump-Xi's Lavish Banquet, Huaiyang Cuisine To Set Tone For Diplomacy

media:content.media:description: Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping will sit down to a lavish state banquet in Beijing on Thursday

item.description: For decades, China has used the powerful symbolism of food during landmark official events and when receiving distinguished foreign visitors.
item.content:encoded: For decades, China has used the powerful symbolism of food during landmark official events and when receiving distinguished foreign visitors.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Trump-Xi Meeting, And The Problem With Dismissing America Too Soon
item.description: Americs is bruised, but appearances can be deceptive in great power politics.
item.content:encoded: Americs is bruised, but appearances can be deceptive in great power politics.

item.title: Who Is Golshifteh Farahani? Actor Linked To Emmanuel Macron In 'Slap' Row

media:content.media:description: Golshifteh Farahani left Iran in 2008 after facing restrictions

item.description: Paris Match political correspondent Florian Tardif suggested the incident followed a jealous argument after Brigitte Macron saw messages exchanged between Emmanuel Macron and Farahani.
item.content:encoded: Paris Match political correspondent Florian Tardif suggested the incident followed a jealous argument after Brigitte Macron saw messages exchanged between Emmanuel Macron and Farahani.

item.title: India Calls For UN Reforms, Gaza Ceasefire At BRICS Foreign Ministers' Meet

media:content.media:description: Jaishankar reserved some of his sharpest language for the threat of terrorism.

item.description: Jaishankar opened his address with a frank assessment of a deteriorating global landscape. Conflicts, climate events, and the Covid pandemic, he said, had been "key contributory factors" to widening vulnerabilities.
item.content:encoded: Jaishankar opened his address with a frank assessment of a deteriorating global landscape. Conflicts, climate events, and the Covid pandemic, he said, had been "key contributory factors" to widening vulnerabilities.

item.title: China's Door Will Open Wider To US Businesses: Xi Jinping Tells US CEOs

media:content.media:description: Neither US or China is looking for a breakthrough. They are looking to avoid another breakdown.

item.description: Trump-Xi Summit: The US President wants China to stop weaponising supply chains, especially rare earths that power US tech and defence industries.
item.content:encoded: Trump-Xi Summit: The US President wants China to stop weaponising supply chains, especially rare earths that power US tech and defence industries.

item.title: Ship Anchored Off UAE Coast Seized, Heads Towards Iranian Waters

media:content.media:description: The British Military confirmed the seizure of the ship. (Representational Image)

item.description: The seizure comes as US President Donald Trump is meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping on a much-anticipated visit to Beijing.
item.content:encoded: The seizure comes as US President Donald Trump is meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping on a much-anticipated visit to Beijing.

item.title: Why Trump's Delegation Entered China Without Personal Phones

media:content.media:description: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping visit the Temple of Heaven in Beijing

item.description: They have chosen to take restricted “clean” devices, temporary laptops and controlled communication systems designed to reduce risks of surveillance, hacking and data interception in China
item.content:encoded: They have chosen to take restricted “clean” devices, temporary laptops and controlled communication systems designed to reduce risks of surveillance, hacking and data interception in China

item.title: Microsoft's $1 Billion Kenya Data Centre Project Halts Over Payment: Report

media:content.media:description: The planned facility, announced in 2024, was positioned as a $1 billion geothermal-powered data centre

item.description: Microsoft and its Abu Dhabi-based partner G42 had sought commitments from the Kenyan government to guarantee annual payments for a fixed amount of computing capacity.
item.content:encoded: Microsoft and its Abu Dhabi-based partner G42 had sought commitments from the Kenyan government to guarantee annual payments for a fixed amount of computing capacity.

item.title: 'Tehran Will Never Bow': Iran's Araghchi Blasts US, Israel At BRICS Meet

media:content.media:description: Araghchi laid out a direct set of demands for the BRICS bloc

item.description: The US-Israeli strikes, Araghchi said, had been defended with "false claims that run counter to the informed assessments of the International Atomic Energy Agency and even America's own intelligence community."
item.content:encoded: The US-Israeli strikes, Araghchi said, had been defended with "false claims that run counter to the informed assessments of the International Atomic Energy Agency and even America's own intelligence community."
